title: Bulk report - Week 10
summary:
Capesize
A stronger week overall for the sector as the North Atlantic appeared active with more enquiry since the middle of the week. With fresh tonnage getting tighter the time charter trips for both fronthaul and Transatlantic runs had the biggest improvement amongst all routes, finishing the week at $16,639 and $29,344 respectively. The Pacific kept its steady pace as the West Australia to Qingdao run stepped up to $8.375, a peak so far this year and last seen before Christmas last year.

children.title: Capesize
children.summary: A stronger week overall for the sector as the North Atlantic appeared active with more enquiry since the middle of the week. With fresh tonnage getting tighter the time charter trips for both fronthaul and Transatlantic runs had the biggest improvement amongst all routes, finishing the week at $16,639 and $29,344 respectively.

children.title: Panamax
children.summary: A captivating week for the Panamax market with various peaks and troughs seen across all markets. The Transatlantic in the North appeared a little nervous in places with a lack of demand and some voyage fixtures equating to exceptionally low returns.

children.title: Ultramax/Supramax
children.summary: A story of two halves, with the start of the week seeing little excitement in Asia at the beginning. However, as the week closed an upturn in fresh cargo enquiry from Indonesia and Australia, combined with a steady supply of backhaul requirements, saw renewed optimism.

children.title: Handysize
children.summary: The buoyant mood continued during the week with positive gains in both basins despite a lack of visible activity. East Coast South America was said to have seen fresh grain enquiry enter the market daily.
